commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=be55341c9ba3bfbab9ee29adf7921b8938f6b7fb)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds WhatsApp interactive list extraction, structured list context, a gateway-backed `list-reply` message action, docs, outbound guards, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +733, Tests +1139, Docs +1. Total +1873 across 31 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection plus Baileys 7.0.0-rc13 package source show button-backed prompts are exposed as list rows while the only send implementation emits `listReply`. The overall PR is a new feature rather than a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer review for the public action/API contract, button-backed prompt scope, and conflict resolution; this is not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): support list reply actions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp interactive list prompts expose stable row ids, but agents could not see or select them reliably.
- Why it matters: business and service flows often require a real WhatsApp `singleSelectReply.selectedRowId`, not a plain-text answer.
- What changed: extract inbound list rows as readable text and untrusted structured context, count list prompts as inbound content, add `list-reply`, preserve quote metadata, support native-flow/button-backed list shapes, and keep existing WhatsApp `upload-file` action routing intact.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol change, no auth or credential behavior change, and no config schema change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83217
- Related #73823
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp native-flow/list prompts are extracted into `Options:` rows with `rowId`, and the agent can answer them with `message(action="list-reply")`.

Real environment tested: Live linked WhatsApp session against Meta's public Jasper's Market sample and a second real WhatsApp Business list flow with identifiers redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Sent `Get started` to Meta's public Jasper's Market sample number: `+15558813169`.
   Public test link: https://api.whatsapp.com/send/?phone=15558813169&text=Get+started&type=phone_number&app_absent=0
2. Ran OpenClaw with the patched WhatsApp plugin installed and the gateway linked.
3. Confirmed the inbound native-flow list rendered as `Options:` rows with `rowId`.
4. Let the agent choose a visible option using `message(action="list-reply")`.

Equivalent OpenClaw trigger command:

```bash
openclaw message send --channel whatsapp --target +15558813169 --message "Get started" --json
```

Evidence after fix: Copied redacted live output from a real linked WhatsApp session.

Video proof using Meta's public Jasper's Market sample flow only:

Before:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09d1206a-12ed-4c0e-a4ee-699c95fd7cdc

After:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/993dece2-bd9b-4286-bb99-e3ee9488fc39

The before video shows the public Jasper's Market WhatsApp list prompt being treated as plain text, leading to normal `message(action="send")` replies. The after video shows OpenClaw exposing `Options:` with `rowId`, the agent calling `message(action="list-reply")`, and the gateway sending a native WhatsApp list reply. No private business flow is included.

```text
Inbound prompt:
Welcome to Jasper's Market! What can we help you with today?
Options:
1. Shop online (rowId: reply-interactive-with-media)
2. Get recipe ideas (rowId: reply-media-card-carousel)
3. Current promo (rowId: reply-offer)

Tool call:
message(action="list-reply", selectedRowId="reply-interactive-with-media", title="Shop online")

Tool result:
{ "ok": true, "selectedRowId": "reply-interactive-with-media" }
```

Additional redacted live list-flow proof:

```text
Inbound prompt:
Options:
1. [redacted visible list option] (rowId: [redacted-row-id])
2. [redacted visible list option] (rowId: [redacted-row-id])

Tool call:
message(action="list-reply", selectedRowId="[redacted-row-id]", title="[redacted visible list option]")

Tool result:
{ "ok": true, "selectedRowId": "[redacted-row-id]" }
```

Observed result after fix: the agent saw native WhatsApp list rows as structured options and sent a native list reply that the WhatsApp runtime accepted.

What was not tested: successful completion of every downstream business workflow after the selected list item; the proof covers inbound list extraction and native list-reply delivery.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #83217 documents the missing current-main support and includes example WhatsApp list screenshots.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm build`

Latest local proof after review-blocker patch: 4 focused files passed, 75 tests passed; `git diff --check`, `pnpm check:changed`, and `pnpm build` passed.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A. This is a new WhatsApp feature request rather than a regression in existing behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts`, `src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts`, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: direct and wrapped list prompt extraction, native-flow list extraction, structured context propagation, content gating, canonical message action schema, row id/title validation, outbound list/button reply payload shape, quote metadata preservation, upload-file routing preservation, plugin-owned aliases, and redacted outbound list-reply logs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior spans plugin extraction, action discovery/dispatch, and the Baileys send seam without needing a live WhatsApp account for deterministic payload proof.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A before this change.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

WhatsApp now advertises and supports a native `list-reply` message action for inbound list prompts. Agents should pass `selectedRowId` and the visible row `title`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
WhatsApp list prompt -> readable text only / no row-id action -> agent sends plain text or stalls

After:
WhatsApp list prompt -> readable options + untrusted structured context -> list-reply action -> WhatsApp singleSelectReply.selectedRowId
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This adds a WhatsApp `list-reply` message action under the existing message tool. It is gated by the existing WhatsApp `actions.sendMessage` gate plus account/allowlist authorization, requires an explicit `selectedRowId` and visible `title`, treats inbound list context as untrusted, keeps target aliases plugin-owned, and avoids logging row titles, descriptions, or selected row ids.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: local developer environment (redacted)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): WhatsApp channel test configs and mocked active listener/send APIs

### Steps

1. Run the focused WhatsApp/action tests listed above.
2. Run `git diff --check`.
3. Run `pnpm check:changed`.
4. Run `pnpm build`.
5. For public live reproduction, message Jasper's Market at `+15558813169` with `Get started`, then verify the agent sees `Options:` rows and uses `message(action="list-reply")`.

### Expected

- Tests pass, changed checks pass, build passes, no whitespace errors are reported, and live list prompts expose selectable `rowId` values.

### Actual

- All listed local commands exited 0. Redacted live output showed the Jasper native-flow prompt rendered with `Options:` and a `list-reply` tool result returned `ok: true`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: inbound list extraction including native-flow and button-backed prompts, list response text rendering, message action discovery/schema, action dispatch, outbound list/button reply payload shapes, quote metadata preservation, upload-file routing preservation, plugin-owned target aliases, SDK export guardrails, docs entry, public Jasper's Market video proof, and redacted live WhatsApp Business sample flow logs.
- Edge cases checked: missing selected row id, missing title, missing target, disabled `sendMessage` action, LID-backed quote metadata, unsupported camelCase alias, wrapped list messages, native-flow list prompts, button-backed prompts, synthetic button/template/native-flow response text, and list-reply log redaction.
- What you did **not** verify: a broad matrix of other WhatsApp Business providers, group chats, and every native-flow button variant beyond the covered list/button shapes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Live WhatsApp Business list flows may require provider-specific nuance not captured by mocked Baileys tests.
  - Mitigation: The implementation uses Baileys' native `listReply`/`buttonReply` payload shapes, preserves quote metadata, keeps the action behind existing send gating, and includes focused seam tests around payload construction plus public Jasper sample proof.
